claims.claim_text: A field trial measured up to a 25% yield decline when oilseed rape is grown in continuous or short rotation versus first-time (virgin) oilseed rape, quantifying why oilseeds should not be grown in close rotation with themselves.
claims.evidence_text: This field trial showed a yield reduction in the continuous OSR (grown for four consecutive years) of 25% compared with virgin OSR. Reported yield decline vary from 3–12% in short rotation to 25% in continuous cropping.

provenance.source_title: Impact of Shortened Crop Rotation of Oilseed Rape on Soil and Rhizosphere Microbial Diversity in Relation to Yield Decline (PLOS ONE)
